newsSince: No new thesis-changing catalyst since June 9. The relevant data points (record Q1 driven by LEO satellite/AI PCB, and the May 28 shareholder-meeting guidance that AI infrastructure will drive sharp 2027-2028 revenue/profit gains) are pre-window and already priced. The stock fell ~12% over the two weeks as the satellite-narrative momentum unwound.

sources.label: DIGITIMES - Compeq AI infra to drive 2027-2028 gains
DIGITIMES - Compeq key supplier in AI and LEO satellite boom

verdicts.rankRationale: Residual-alpha lens: no fresh bottleneck evidence since June 9; the satellite/AI-PCB narrative that drove the prior limit-up has unwound (-12%). Thesis is unchanged but the hidden-upside case remains thin at +157% YTD with the prior down verdict already digesting the crowding. Small thesis fade, hold rank.
verdicts.bull: Core LEO-satellite and AI-server HDI/PCB supplier; AI infra to lift complexity, reliability and gross margins into 2027-2028.
verdicts.neutral: Real AI/satellite content, but the big 2027-2028 payoff is back-end-loaded and the May guidance is already known.
verdicts.bear: Underfollowed name was repriced on a satellite-news momentum spike; +157% YTD with no fresh catalyst leaves little residual alpha.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Ramp of AI-server and LEO satellite HDI orders into 2027
